mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating consumer spending power, triggering a negative earnings revision cycle that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE breaches the 5.0% threshold within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which amplifies downside risk to corporate profits and drives negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns more often than a random baseline (accuracy < 52% over 3 years).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 210.0, aggressive federal funds rate hikes compress equity multiples by raising the risk-free rate without supporting earnings growth, producing negative forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL remains above the 210.0 threshold over a 2-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income, proxied by sustained growth in retail sales and personal services, directly fuels corporate revenue expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth periods do not yield statistically higher 20-day forward returns compared to contraction periods (t-test p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth decelerates below a 0.2% monthly threshold, it indicates supply chain bottlenecks or weak manufacturing demand, which precedes earnings downgrades and generates negative equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative following months where INDPRO growth falls below the 0.2% threshold over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with a rising federal funds rate creates a dual squeeze on consumer credit and business investment, forcing a sharp repricing of equity risk premiums and driving negative forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint increase of UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns with accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust payroll growth exceeding 150,000 per month, housing starts act as a leading indicator of consumer confidence; a breakdown in housing construction despite strong employment signals a structural demand shift that depresses equity valuations over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline significantly when PAYEMS exceeds 150k but HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average over a 3-year walk-forward test.
